report_name: California Sues 23andMe Over 2023 Breach, Alleging Major Security and Privacy Failures

attack_patterns.name: Credential Stuffing
attack_patterns.description: Attackers used credentials stolen from other data breaches to gain access to approximately 14,000 23andMe accounts over a five-month period in 2023.

attack_patterns.name: Valid Accounts
attack_patterns.description: Attackers successfully authenticated to 14,000 user accounts using compromised credentials obtained through a credential stuffing campaign.

attack_patterns.name: Automated Collection
attack_patterns.description: After gaining access to accounts, attackers exploited the 'DNA Relatives' feature to automatically scrape and exfiltrate the data of 6.9 million users.

campaigns.name: 23andMe Credential Stuffing Campaign 2023
campaigns.description: A large-scale credential stuffing campaign occurring in 2023 that targeted 23andMe, resulting in the compromise of 14,000 accounts and the subsequent scraping of data from 6.9 million users via the 'DNA Relatives' feature.

identities.name: 23andMe
identities.description: A genetic testing company that was the target of a 2023 credential stuffing campaign and is now being sued by the state of California for failing to maintain reasonable security practices.

identities.name: State of California
identities.description: The state government, through its Attorney General's office, filed a lawsuit against 23andMe for violations of state privacy laws, including the CCPA and GIPA, following a 2023 data breach.

identities.name: 23andMe Users
identities.description: A group of 6.9 million individuals whose genetic and personal data was exposed after attackers exploited the 'DNA Relatives' feature during the 2023 credential stuffing attack on 23andMe.

identities.name: California Residents
identities.description: A group of over 850,000 individuals residing in California whose personal information was compromised in the 23andMe breach, triggering CCPA implications.

indicators.name: Login Attempt with Known Pwned Password
indicators.pattern:
title: Login Attempt with Known Pwned Password
id: 2795c18a-1f8d-4b9e-9b2a-0a4e76c1d3f0
status: experimental
description: >
  Detects a log event indicating a user attempted to log in or register with a password that is known to have been exposed in other data breaches. This is a key defense against credential stuffing.
references:
  - https://www.hipaajournal.com/california-ag-files-lawsuit-over-23andme-data-breach/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-29
tags:
  - attack.t1110.004
level: high
logsource:
  category: application
detection:
  keywords:
    - 'password is known to be pwned'
    - 'Authentication attempt with breached credential'
  condition: 1 of keywords
indicators.description: Detects login attempts using passwords known to be compromised, a direct countermeasure to the credential stuffing attack used against 23andMe.

indicators.name: Access to Sensitive Data-Sharing API
indicators.pattern:
title: Access to Sensitive Data-Sharing API Endpoint
id: 8b3d4e5f-6a7b-8901-cdef-123456789012
status: experimental
description: >
  Detects access to a sensitive API endpoint, such as one that returns user relative data. High volume of events from a single source should be correlated in a SIEM to detect potential data scraping.
references:
  - https://www.hipaajournal.com/california-ag-files-lawsuit-over-23andme-data-breach/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-29
tags:
  - attack.t1119
level: low
logsource:
  category: proxy
detection:
  selection:
    c-uri-stem|contains: '/relatives'
    cs-method: 'GET'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects access to an API endpoint consistent with the 'DNA Relatives' feature that was abused for mass data scraping. High volumes of this event from a single source are indicative of an attack.

relationships.source_name: Login Attempt with Known Pwned Password
